title: OS Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter in every <os_dir>/<id>.md file
x-file-example:
---
id: ubuntu_22_04
collection: os
name: "Ubuntu 22.04 LTS"
family: linux
form_factor: server
version: "22.04"
arch:
  - x86_64
  - arm64
kernel: "5.15"
package_manager: apt
eol: 2027-04-01
lts: true
tools:
  - "[[docker]]"
  - "[[pytorch]]"
tags:
  - ubuntu
  - debian-based
---

## Overview
Debian-based Linux distribution with long-term support, widely used in cloud and server environments.

## Notes

id.description: Snake_case ID used as the filename and in [[wikilinks]]

collection.description: Node type — used by Obsidian-style apps to filter nodes by collection

name.description: Display name of the OS including version or codename where applicable

family.description: Kernel lineage / ecosystem. android is separate from linux despite sharing the kernel — it has a distinct userspace and ecosystem.

version.description: Version string as displayed in official docs (e.g. '22.04', 'Ventura', '11')

arch.description: CPU architectures supported (e.g. x86_64, arm64, riscv64)

kernel.description: Kernel version shipped with this OS release (snapshot, not live)

package_manager.description: Default system package manager (e.g. apt, dnf, brew, winget)

eol.description: End-of-life date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD). Null for rolling-release or unknown.

lts.description: Whether this is a long-term support release

tools.description: [[wikilink]] references to tool nodes that support this OS
